title: Commitment vs. Re-planning

runtime.expected_outcome: Replanning every step is safer; committing to 2+ steps is faster. Recipe shows the tradeoff in expected regret.

symbols.G*: best policy under fully re-planned trajectory

audiences.kid: Do you decide every move, or plan two moves ahead and only rethink then? Depends on how much the world surprises you.
audiences.real: Replanning-every-step is robust to noise. Committing to k>1 actions saves compute but can be costly if observations reveal the committed plan is wrong.
audiences.equation: Expected regret of commitment grows with env stochasticity and policy_depth. Tunable by Application.
audiences.derivation: Commitment trades expected regret for compute savings. Optimal commitment depth k* solves argmin_k compute(k) + regret(k).

orchestrate.objective: Show the commitment-vs-replanning tradeoff on a simple maze.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns; run SophisticatedPlan with commit in {1, 2, 3}.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND).
